questions.q: The process of replacing a halogen atom in an aromatic compound with an amino group is called?
Which of the following haloalkanes undergoes substitution reaction?
Write the IUPAC name of the compound (CH_(3)CH_(2)CH_(2)CH_(2)Cl)?
Explain the difference between electrophilic substitution and nucleophilic substitution reactions of haloarenes?
questions.answer: The difference between electrophilic substitution and nucleophilic substitution reactions is that in electrophilic substitution, an electrophile attacks the ring to replace the halogen atom, whereas in nucleophilic substitution, a nucleophile attacks the carbon-halogen bond to replace the halogen atom.

questions.q: What is the major product formed when bromobenzene is treated with CH_(3)CH_(2)MgBr?
Which of the following is not a method for the preparation of haloarenes?
Write the mechanism of the reaction between chlorobenzene and KOH?
questions.answer: The mechanism of the reaction between chlorobenzene and KOH involves the attack of the hydroxide ion on the carbon-chlorine bond, followed by the elimination of HCl.

questions.q: What is the effect of increasing the temperature on the rate of reaction in the electrophilic substitution reaction?
questions.answer: Increasing the temperature increases the rate of reaction in the electrophilic substitution reaction.

questions.q: Which of the following haloarenes is most stable?
Explain the concept of directing effect in haloarenes?
questions.answer: The directing effect in haloarenes refers to the ability of a substituent on the benzene ring to direct the incoming electrophile. Electron-donating groups (such as -OH, -NH_(2)) are ortho/para directors, while electron-withdrawing groups (such as -NO_(2), -COOH) are meta directors.
